phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the flower market opens
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure ouvre le marché aux fleurs ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UHR OO-vreh leh mar-SHAY oh FLUHR
phrases.cultural_note: The flower market runs Tuesday–Sunday mornings. Monday is the antiques market. Everything closes around 1pm.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the price of a bunch of mimosa
phrases.text_local: C'est combien pour un bouquet de mimosa, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: say kom-BYAN poor un boo-KAY deh mee-mo-ZAH seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Mimosa is the flower symbol of the Riviera, blooming brilliantly in February and sold throughout the markets.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find locally grown vegetables
phrases.text_local: Où sont les stands de légumes cultivés localement ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo son lay STAND deh lay-GYOOM kul-tee-VAY lo-kal-MAN
phrases.cultural_note: Cours Saleya mixes flower stalls, fresh produce, local cheeses, olives and Niçois specialties all in one lively square.

responses.english: The vegetables are on the other side.
responses.phonetic: lay lay-GOOM sohn duh LOH-truh koh-TAY
responses.local: Les légumes sont de l'autre côté.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a vendor if they have fresh tapenade
phrases.text_local: Avez-vous de la tapenade fraîche, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY voo deh lah tah-peh-NAHD FRESH seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Tapenade — an olive and caper paste — is a Provençal staple. Fresh market versions are far better than jarred imports.

responses.phonetic: wee zhahn ay duh lah FRESH
responses.local: Oui, j'en ai de la fraîche.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if you can taste before buying
phrases.text_local: Est-il possible de goûter avant d'acheter ?
phrases.pronunciation: eh-TEEL po-SEEBL deh goo-TAY ah-VAN dah-sheh-TAY
phrases.cultural_note: Most cheese and olive vendors gladly offer tastings — it's expected and part of the market culture.
